weather_line: Overcast in the hollows · second bookmark weather

stories.headline: Steve Briefs Christine on the Second Bookmark; She Has Already Asked Four Clarifying Questions and Requested the Elevation Data
body: The briefing, which Steve had been conducting quietly inside his own head for approximately three days, became official last evening when Christine located the second bookmark herself — pressed into a fold of the trail map forty miles east of Jellico, in a crease he had not yet named aloud. Steve confirmed its existence. Christine asked where the ridge ran, what the approach looked like, whether the canopy broke anywhere interesting, and — before he had answered the first question — what the elevation change was on the eastern face.

Steve answered all four in order, which sources close to the expedition describe as 'his best showing since the Hollow Crown briefing.' The Jeep, informed of the exchange via the usual channels, has already rotated its mirrors to the optimum trail angle and is understood to be waiting.
No departure date has been announced. The second bookmark has, however, been moved from the crease to a flat page, which the Adventure Desk regards as significant.

stories.headline: Election Board Convenes; Ragnok Stands Straighter Than He Has in Thirty Years; Griphook the Younger Requests Another Recess Before Proceedings Have Begun
body: The Gringotts Election Board convened Thursday morning in the Third Sub-Chamber, security upgraded following last year's incident with the enchanted abacus. Ragnok arrived four minutes early — a personal record — with Christine's reference folded into his left breast pocket, pressed flat. He has not opened it since Wednesday but has touched it twice.

Griphook the Younger arrived on time, requested a recess at ten past the hour on procedural grounds, was denied, and requested a second recess on different procedural grounds. He was denied again. The Board's presiding officer, a goblin so elderly she is listed in two separate centuries of Gringotts records, told him he could have a recess when he produced a rule that actually existed. The Chamber fell very quiet.

Christine, who had submitted her reference and departed, was reported to be at her desk resolving an entirely separate vault dispute before the Board had called order. The goblins, per usual, filed a complaint — this time about her efficiency.

stories.headline: Thursday Achieves Midweek Equilibrium; Household Confirms All Three Departments Operating Within Acceptable Parameters
body: A thorough survey of the household conducted Thursday afternoon found all systems nominal. The kitchen reported adequate provisions. The warmest lap in the house — a position whose governance has not changed in some time — remained fully occupied and showed no signs of opening to public competition.

The only anomaly noted was a biscuit discrepancy in the pantry, consistent with patterns the Home Bureau has documented across eleven previous editions without, as yet, producing a single confirmed suspect.

stories.headline: Mysterious Glowing Briefcase Resurfaces in Level Three Corridor; Ministry Confirms It Is 'Not a Concern' in Statement That Raises Every Concern
body: The briefcase, last documented in the Level Six corridor two weeks prior, was observed Thursday morning on Level Three, unattended, emanating its customary amber glow and emitting what one witness described as 'a hum you feel rather than hear.' Ministry spokespeople issued a statement at noon confirming that the briefcase is not a concern, that it is being monitored by the appropriate department, and that no one should open it.
The appropriate department, when contacted, said they were aware of it. They declined to specify in what sense they were aware of it. The briefcase remained in the corridor as of the evening edition.

familiars_corner: Lucy: The biscuit discrepancy noted in the Home Bureau's Thursday report remains, as always, entirely circumstantial — Lucy wishes to remind the household that circumstantial is a legal category, not a moral one, and she has retained no counsel because she requires none.

Ellie: Ellie spent a portion of Thursday afternoon introducing herself to the back door, which she has passed through approximately four hundred times but greeted today as though meeting it for the first time; the door, to its credit, was very welcoming.
Jersey: The lap summit of Wednesday has extended, without formal adjournment, into Thursday; Jersey considers this simply good governance and sees no reason to call a recess.
founding_editor_note: That Election Board chamber — someone built that Sub-Chamber crooked, you can see it in the arch. I'd have fixed it in a weekend. — D.

in_brief.sport: Thursday Strength Report: Christine lifted the entire weight of the Gringotts Election Board's procedural calendar before breakfast; no new records announced, but the Board has been warned.
in_brief.forecast: Overcast through the hollows, clearing east — ideal conditions for consulting a second bookmark and asking four clarifying questions about the elevation data.

storyline_updates.title: The Second Bookmark
storyline_updates.summary: Christine has been fully briefed on the second bookmark — she found it herself and asked four clarifying questions before Steve finished answering the first. He answered all four. The Jeep's mirrors are set. No departure date is set but the bookmark has been moved to a flat page, which the Adventure Desk considers the last step before an announcement. Day 3 should cover either a departure date being named, or the first real detail about what the fold forty miles east of Jellico actually contains — what Steve saw there, or heard about, that made it worth two bookmarks.
